meta.name: Hummus (Classic Levantine Chickpea-Tahini Dip)
meta.subtitle: Ultra-smooth, lemon-bright, garlic-forward; finished with olive oil and paprika/sumac.

source.name: ssa.catalogs
notes: Canonical SSA recipe: classic Levantine hummus bi tahini.
Designed for deterministic planners and adapter pipeline (equipment + doneness neutral).
Ingredient IDs are stable catalog-style strings (no schema-enforced pattern).

yield.notes: Makes ~2 to 2.5 cups (approx. 480–600 g) depending on thickness.

required.label: Food processor (preferred) or blender
required.notes: For ultra-smooth hummus, a processor works best; blender can work with extra liquid and scraping.

optional.label: Pressure cooker
optional.notes: Optional to cook chickpeas quickly if starting from dry.

optional.label: Microplane / fine grater
optional.notes: For grating garlic very fine to avoid harsh chunks.

notes: Contains sesame (tahini). May contain traces depending on tahini processing facility.

ingredients.label: Chickpeas (canned), drained and rinsed
notes: This equals about 2 standard 15-oz / 425 g cans drained.
If using home-cooked chickpeas, measure about 3 cups cooked (~450 g cooked).

ingredients.label: Aquafaba (chickpea liquid) or cold water
notes: Start with less; add gradually until creamy.
Aquafaba adds silkiness and helps emulsify.

ingredients.label: Tahini (sesame paste), well stirred
notes: Use a runny, pourable tahini for best texture.
If tahini is bitter, balance with a little extra lemon and salt.

ingredients.label: Fresh lemon juice
notes: About 1.5–2 lemons depending on size/juiciness.

ingredients.label: Garlic cloves, finely grated or minced
notes: Adjust to taste; raw garlic strength varies.
For a softer garlic note, briefly soak minced garlic in lemon juice for 5 minutes.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Start at ~1 tsp kosher; adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin
notes: Optional but common; enhances hummus aroma without making it taste like chili.

ingredients.label: Baking soda
notes: Optional trick for extra-smooth hummus: briefly simmer chickpeas with baking soda to loosen skins.
Skip if you prefer quicker prep.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
notes: For serving drizzle. Do not blend too much olive oil into hummus; it can dull the flavor.

steps.title: Mise en place
instructions: Juice the lemon and grate/mince the garlic very finely.
Stir tahini thoroughly so it is smooth and pourable.
checks: Tahini is fully mixed (no separated oil).
Garlic is fine enough to disappear into the hummus.

steps.title: Optional: soften chickpeas for extra-smooth hummus
instructions: For extra-smooth hummus, add rinsed chickpeas to a small pot with enough water to cover by 1 inch.
Add baking soda (optional). Bring to a gentle simmer for 8–10 minutes until chickpeas look slightly overcooked and skins loosen.
Drain and rinse under cool water. Rub gently to remove loose skins (optional).
checks: Chickpeas are soft and skins loosen easily (optional).
Chickpeas are well drained before blending.

steps.title: Whip tahini + lemon into a creamy base
instructions: In a food processor, add tahini and lemon juice. Process 30–60 seconds until thickened and pale.
Add garlic, salt, and cumin (optional). Process 15–20 seconds to combine.
checks: Mixture becomes lighter in color and slightly fluffy.

steps.title: Blend chickpeas until smooth
instructions: Add half the chickpeas to the processor. Process 45–60 seconds, scrape down the sides.
Add remaining chickpeas. Process 60–90 seconds, scraping as needed, until thick and mostly smooth.
With the processor running, drizzle in aquafaba or cold water a tablespoon at a time until creamy and spreadable.
checks: Hummus holds soft peaks and spreads easily.

steps.title: Adjust seasoning and brightness
instructions: Taste and adjust: add more salt for flavor, lemon for brightness, or a splash of water for looseness.
If garlic is sharp, let hummus sit 5 minutes and re-taste (flavors mellow).
checks: Balanced salt + lemon (bright but not sour).

steps.title: Serve Levantine-style
instructions: Spoon hummus into a shallow bowl and swirl with the back of a spoon.
Drizzle with olive oil. Sprinkle paprika or sumac and parsley if using.
Serve with warm pita, cucumbers, tomatoes, olives, or as part of a mezze spread.
checks: Olive oil on top (not blended in).
Garnish added lightly to avoid overpowering.

notes: Press plastic wrap directly on the surface to reduce drying.
If it thickens, stir in 1–2 tbsp water and a squeeze of lemon to refresh.
Texture may change slightly after thawing; re-whip in processor with a splash of water/lemon.
Freeze in small portions for easy thaw.

prepNotes: Pairs well with grilled meats/veg and fresh salads.
leftoversPlan: Thin with lemon + water for dressing

notes: Keep refrigerated if not serving immediately.
Do not leave at room temperature longer than 2 hours (1 hour if very warm).
For ultra-smooth hummus: warm chickpeas (or softened) + whip tahini/lemon first + blend longer than you think.
If tahini is very thick, add extra cold water gradually to prevent seize.
Aleppo pepper can replace paprika for gentle warmth.

add.label: Roasted red peppers, drained
notes: Blend in with chickpeas.
Reduce added water slightly; peppers add moisture.

add.label: Roasted beets, peeled
notes: Blend in with chickpeas for a magenta color and earthy sweetness.
Add an extra squeeze of lemon to keep it bright.
Soak minced garlic in lemon juice 5 minutes before blending to soften harshness.
